sourceEvidence:
Computer Engineering K-Companies Exam - Questions.pdf, p.41 (question page) prints verbatim:
"18. An output device that uses words or messages recorded on a magnetic medium to produce audio response is
b. Magnetic tape
c. Voice response unit
d. Voice recognition unit
e. Voice band"
There is NO option 'a' on the page. Verified by rendering p.41 at 400 dpi and thresholding the crop: the stem line is followed directly by 'b. Magnetic tape', with the same inter-option leading as between b/c, c/d and d/e. No blank, no faint line, no dropped option.
The same PDF's answer section (p.45) prints:
"18. An output device that uses words or messages recorded on a magnetic medium to produce audio response is
b. Voice response unit"
note: Not our extraction's fault and no option was dropped: the paper itself letters this question's four options b-e instead of a-d, and our item reproduces the source verbatim (stem, 4 option texts, labels). The item is answerable and the shipped key (Voice response unit) matches the paper's answer page. Separate observation for the key/label pass, NOT touched here: the paper's answer page letters the correct option 'b' while its question page letters the same option 'c', which indicates the question page's b-e run is the paper's own lettering typo for a canonical a-d list (a. Magnetic tape / b. Voice response unit / c. Voice recognition unit / d. Voice band); any renumbering to A-D is out of scope for this pass since corrected labels must match existing ones.
